candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line below gross profit and alongside operating expense rows, but not explicitly tied to cost of goods sold / cost of revenues. Relative to the selected COGS row, this looks outside the COGS bucket rather than a COGS-specific add-back.
Separate operating depreciation lines appear below gross profit and are not labelled as being in COGS. They look more like operating overhead components than COGS-specific D&A.
Separate operating amortisation line appears outside the selected COGS row and is not identified as cost-of-sales amortisation.

variables.notes: No explicit D&A row is identified as belonging to COGS / cost of sales. The visible D&A lines sit as separate operating lines below gross profit, so they should not be added to the selected COGS bucket.

candidates.why: Explicit separate D&A line in the operating section, at the same general level as SG&A rather than nested beneath it. Relative to the selected 'Selling, General & Administrative Expenses' row, this appears to sit outside the selected SG&A bucket and therefore is the best summary D&A row to add separately where reported.
Separate operating depreciation lines are shown outside the SG&A row. They may represent the component detail of D&A in periods where the combined summary line is not reported.
Separate operating amortisation line is shown outside the SG&A row and appears to be another D&A component at the operating level.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit combined D&A summary row to avoid double counting against component rows. Manual review is warranted because the statement also shows separate 'Depreciation' and 'Amortisation' rows in other periods, so the presentation may vary by year and the single summary row may not capture all periods consistently.
